message:
fix(supply_chain): correct inverted safety tier mapping in revocation integration

The RevocationIntegrationPolicy and RevocationIntegrationEngine had
the medium and high extension safety tier thresholds swapped:

- high_tier_max_age_secs was 60*60 (1 hour) but should be 5*60 (5 min)
  since high-risk extensions need the strictest freshness requirement
- medium_tier_max_age_secs was 5*60 (5 min) but should be 60*60 (1 hour)

Similarly, the FreshnessPolicy mapping was inverted:
- High extension tier was mapped to SafetyTier::Risky (lenient)
  instead of SafetyTier::Dangerous (strict)
- Medium extension tier was mapped to SafetyTier::Dangerous (strict)
  instead of SafetyTier::Risky (lenient)

This meant high-risk extensions were getting the most lenient
revocation freshness checks while medium-risk ones got the strictest —
the exact opposite of the intended security posture.

Also add missing Medium tier assertion in default_policy_has_positive_max_ages
test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
